---
title: "Academy — The Comic Tour"
subtitle: "45 minutes to the mental model, eight images at a time"
date: today
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY"
description: "The eight narrative comics that top the UIAO_128 Education funnel. One image per abstraction, one short read, one companion canonical source."
aspirational: true
---

Before you pick a [Contributor](contributor-track.qmd) or [Operator](operator-track.qmd) track, build the mental model. The **comics** are the top of the UIAO_128 Education funnel — one image per core abstraction. Roughly 45 minutes cover-to-cover if you pause on each panel; 5 minutes if you flip through them as a flyover.

Each comic below pairs with a short narrative walkthrough and a canonical source. The walkthrough grounds the image; the canonical source names the spec the abstraction is governed by.

::: {.callout-note}
## Where the comics live

The comic JPG assets live under [`docs/publications/series-assets/`](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/tree/main/docs/publications/series-assets). Each row below names the specific asset.
:::

---

## 1. The Governance OS

**What it shows:** UIAO is an operating system for governance, not a scanner, not a document generator.

**Walkthrough:** [docs/narrative/program-education.qmd — "Before you can use UIAO…"](../narrative/program-education.qmd)

**Canon anchor:** the whole substrate — start with [UIAO_001 SSOT](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/UIAO-SSOT.md).

---

## 2. The Canonical Object

**What it shows:** one object, one canonical record. Every telemetry event, policy decision, enforcement action attaches to a canonical object.

**Canon anchor:** [UIAO_001 SSOT](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/UIAO-SSOT.md) + [UIAO_129 Application Identity Model](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/application-identity-model.md) — the application is the first-class canonical object.

---

## 3. The Unified Environment Map

**What it shows:** adapters collapse many vendor surfaces into one map. The reader stops thinking "which vendor?" and starts thinking "which application?"

**Canon anchor:** [UIAO_003 Adapter Segmentation Overview](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/UIAO_003_Adapter_Segmentation_Overview_v1.0.md) + [modernization-registry.yaml](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/modernization-registry.yaml).

---

## 4. Provenance

**What it shows:** every artifact traces back to a canonical source. No orphan evidence, no free-floating claims.

**Canon anchor:** [UIAO_113 Evidence Graph Model](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/graph-schema.md).

---

## 5. The Modernization Lifecycle

**What it shows:** a modernization is not a project — it's a lifecycle. UIAO runs it end-to-end: identify → plan → execute → verify → drift-watch → remediate.

**Canon anchor:** [UIAO_124 Adapter Operations Runbook](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/adapter-operations-runbook.md) + [UIAO_127 Project Plans Program](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/program-project-plans.md).

---

## 6. Address Governance

**What it shows:** the directory / IPAM flow that once lived in the `uiao-gos` predecessor repo. IP addresses, DNS zones, and canonical identity stay coherent across a migration.

**Walkthrough:** [Governance-OS directory migration narrative](../narrative/governance-os-directory-migration.md)

**Canon anchor:** [ADR-028](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/adr/adr-028-monorepo-consolidation-gos-integration.md) + modernization-registry entries for `bluecat-address-manager`, `infoblox-nios`.

---

## 7. Drift Detection

**What it shows:** the closed loop — scan → classify → remediate. Canon says what should be; walker says what is; the delta is a drift finding.

**Canon anchor:** [UIAO_110 Drift Engine](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/drift.md) + the five-class drift taxonomy in [16_DriftDetectionStandard](../docs/16_DriftDetectionStandard.qmd).

---

## 8. Drift Remediation

**What it shows:** a finding becomes a POA&M item, then a closed ticket. The loop doesn't just detect — it resolves, and the resolution is audit-grade.

**Canon anchor:** [UIAO_117 Recovery Layer](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/recovery.md) + OSCAL POA&M outputs.

---

## When you're ready

Having seen the eight abstractions, pick a track:

- [Contributor track](contributor-track.qmd) — you'll write code and canon
- [Operator track](operator-track.qmd) — you'll run the substrate against your environment

Both start from [UIAO_125 Training Program](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/program-training.md)'s shared core. Pick by role, not by depth.
